flags.concern: Timeline overhang math: Step 1's overhang formula ignores clip speed, but this editor derives timeline span from source span divided by playback speed. Any non-1x clip would get the wrong dead-tail width, divider position, and trim amount if the helper uses `to - assetDuration` directly.
flags.evidence: The plan's Step 1 defines overhang as `to - assetDuration`. In the repo, clip timeline duration is `getClipSourceDuration(clip) / speed` in `src/tools/video-editor/lib/config-utils.ts:17-27`, and timeline actions are built from that duration in `src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-data.ts:184-188`. Playback speed is also applied at render time in `src/tools/video-editor/compositions/VisualClip.tsx:103-141`.

flags.concern: Pinned-shot-group menu scope: Step 8 puts Trim/Hold on the existing `ClipAction` context menu, but the current menu is intentionally disabled for pinned-shot-group members except generation update/reminder actions. The main overhang entry points in this plan create pinned shot groups, so those clips would not expose the new commands unless the plan also changes the pinned-group menu gating or adds the actions elsewhere.
flags.evidence: Shot drop and switch-to-final-video both create pinned groups via `pinnedShotGroupsOverride` in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.ts:166-177` and `src/tools/video-editor/lib/shot-group-commands.ts:377-390`. In `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ClipAction.tsx:190-266` the normal menu items are wrapped in `!props.isInPinnedShotGroup`, and `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ClipAction.tsx:360-365` only allows the menu to open for pinned clips when stale/update-variant actions exist.

flags.concern: Asset-duration resolution path: Step 4 assumes missing durations will later appear in the asset registry, but the current repo does not have a background retry/backfill path for final-video assets after an initial duration lookup fails. A watcher on `AssetRegistryEntry.duration` is not enough by itself; the implementation also needs a concrete mechanism that retries metadata extraction and writes the resolved duration back into the registry.
flags.evidence: `resolveFinalVideoDurationSeconds()` does one best-effort lookup and returns `null` on failure in `src/tools/video-editor/lib/finalVideoAssets.ts:119-133`. `registerFinalVideoAsset()` immediately persists whatever came back in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useSwitchToFinalVideo.ts:37-46`, and `SupabaseDataProvider.registerAsset()` only stores the provided entry in `src/tools/video-editor/data/SupabaseDataProvider.ts:226-242`. There is no existing later updater that fills in a missing duration for that asset.

flags.concern: Intent tracking for auto-clamp: Step 4 says to auto-clamp bad fallback clips but leave clips alone when the user intentionally extended them. The current data model has no persisted field that distinguishes a guessed 5-second span from a deliberate overhang created while duration was unknown, so that guard is not implementable as written.
flags.evidence: Timeline clips only persist fields such as `from`, `to`, `speed`, and `hold` in `src/tools/video-editor/types/index.ts:100-125`; there is no 'pending duration' or 'intentional hold tail' marker today. The editor already allows oversized visual clips when asset duration is unknown, as covered by `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipResize.test.tsx:328-369`, so a later auto-clamp cannot tell whether an overshoot came from the original fallback or from a user action without new state.
